feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Co-occurring fisheries and tourism synergistically drive behavioural shifts in endangered dolphins
entry.content:
Authors: I Samad, H Patil, M Cantor, D Farine, D Sutaria... - Marine Ecology Progress ..., 2026

Abstract: Intensifying human activities are reshaping marine ecosystems worldwide, and yet their impact on wildlife behaviour and survival remain poorly understood. While fisheries-related mortality is the primary threat to cetaceans globally, many dolphin ...

Alert: Damien Farine (author alert)

entry.title: CP26 is not involved in qE-or qZ-type non-photochemical quenching in Arabidopsis
entry.content:
Authors: J Walter, D Patel-Tupper, L Lam, A Ma, G Taylor... - Plant Physiology, 2026

Abstract: CP26 is a monomeric minor light-harvesting complex of Photosystem II (LHCII) protein located at the interface between LHCII trimers and the PSII core in thylakoid membranes. Previous studies have proposed CP26 plays a role in non ...

Alert: Graham Fleming (author alert)

entry.title: Augpred: Microarray-Based Machine Learning for Heart Transplants Rejection and Infection Detection
entry.content:
Authors: D Perazzolo, I Barison, M Fedrigo, A Angelini, C Basso... - The Journal of Heart and ..., 2026

Abstract: Purpose: Differentiating Cytomegalovirus (CMV) infection from rejection in heart transplant recipients is challenging due to the possible overlapping of some histopathological features. Microarray transcripts profiling offers a promising ...

Alert: Manlio De Domenico (author alert)

entry.title: Multilayer Networks Reveal Centrality Rewiring in Heart Transplant Rejection and Infection
entry.content:
Authors: D Perazzolo, I Barison, M Fedrigo, A Angelini, C Basso... - The Journal of Heart and ..., 2026

Abstract: Purpose: Transcript-level regulatory mechanisms in cardiac allograft vasculopathy, infection, and graft dysfunction remain poorly characterized. Complex network systems can explore the intricate architecture of interactions and capture phenotype ...

Alert: Manlio De Domenico (author alert)

entry.title: Entropy production bounds for systems running computer programs
entry.content:
Authors: A Yadav, F Caravelli, D Wolpert - PNAS Nexus, 2026

Abstract: Mismatch cost (MMC) is a universally applicable lower bound on the entropy production (EP) of any fixed physical process across a given time interval. In the first part of the paper, we establish results concerning MMC to prove that it scales at least ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Complexity

Alert: David H. Wolpert (author alert)

entry.title: Epistemic Pluralism & Astrobiology
entry.content:
Authors: KJ Smith, PD Anderson, ML Wong - Journal for General Philosophy of Science, 2026

Abstract: Astrobiology is a field that asks profound questions about the universe by simultaneously acquiring knowledge from multiple disciplines in order to propel us toward a better understanding of what life is, how it emerges, and if it exists beyond ...

Alert: Michael L Wong (author alert)

entry.title: Processing argument structure in mild, non-fluent aphasia: Preliminary findings from contrasting Spanish with Basque and English
entry.content:
Authors: P Heinzova, M Goral, M Carreiras, S Mancini - International Journal of Speech ..., 2026

Abstract: Purpose People with aphasia have been found to experience more difficulty in producing verbs with a greater number of arguments (transitives vs. intransitives) or non-canonical argument mapping (unaccusatives vs. unergatives). Basque is an ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Language, Numeracy, and Musicality

Alert: Manuel Carreiras (author alert)

entry.title: Optimal algorithmic complexity of inference in quantum kernel methods
entry.content:
Authors: E Gil-Fuster, S Shin, S Jerbi, J Eisert, MJ Kramer - arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.15214, 2026

Abstract: Quantum kernel methods are among the leading candidates for achieving quantum advantage in supervised learning. A key bottleneck is the cost of inference: evaluating a trained model on new data requires estimating a weighted sum $\sum ...

Alert: Jens Eisert (author alert)

entry.title: Received: 5 November 2019/Accepted: 16 April 2020# The Author (s) 2020
entry.content:
Authors: EHF de Haan, PM Corballis, SA Hillyard, CA Marzi...

Abstract: REVIEW Split-Brain: What We Know Now and Why This is Important for Understanding Consciousness Edward HF de Haan1 & Paul M. Corballis2 & Steven A. Hillyard3 & Carlo A. Marzi4 & Anil Seth5 & Victor AF Lamme1 & Lukas Volz6 & Mara Fabri7 & ...
